content: Component architecture for browser-based idle RPG combat: The standard pattern is a Module Pattern with clear separation: (1) CombatStore — holds server state as-is, no transformations. (2) CombatDiff — pure function: (prevState, nextState) => AnimationStep[]. (3) AnimationQueue — processes steps sequentially/parallel, fires callbacks on completion. (4) DOMRenderer — applies CSS classes, creates/removes DOM elements for particles/projectiles, updates HP bars. (5) FloatTextManager — manages floating damage/heal numbers as a separate subsystem with its own queue. (6) ParticleManager — manages projectile trails and explosion particles with RAF-based animation for smooth movement. For vanilla JS without a framework, these are typically single-instance singletons (object literals or IIFE modules). The key boundary: AnimationQueue knows nothing about the DOM; DOMRenderer knows nothing about timing. This allows testing animation logic without browser APIs.
